zh.lifeSummary: Constance VAN IEPER VAN LOON ou d'Ypres於大約1026年出生在比利時維斯吉福拉安德倫易也。她和Wauthier De Bailleul至少有4個兒子和2個女兒。她去世於1094年在法國豪-德-福朗斯索姆貝爾爾享年69歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Constance VAN IEPER VAN LOON ou d'Ypres于大约1026年出生在比利时維斯吉福拉安德倫易也。她和Wauthier De Bailleul至少有4个儿子和2个女儿。她去世于1094年在法国豪-德-福朗斯索姆貝爾爾享年69岁。

nameMeanings.name: VAN
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: IEPER
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Erwin, Hans, Alois, Armin, Bernhard, Ernst, Fritz, Gerhard, Gunter, Heinz.
North German and Dutch: occupational name for a piper, a town musician, from Middle Low German piper, Middle Dutch pi(j)per. Compare Piper 4 and German Pfeiffer .
Germanized form of Sorbian Pipar: nickname or occupational name derived from pipa ‘pipe (for smoking)’.

variants.name: Piper
meanings: English (mainly southern): occupational name for a player on the pipes, Middle English pipere, sometimes a paid town musician.
English: sometimes a variant of   Peppard  .
English: perhaps occasionally an occupational name from late Middle English piper ‘repairer or layer of pipes; plumber’.

variants.name: Peeper
meanings: Dutch: probably a variant of Dutch Peper, a metonymic occupational name for a spice merchant, from peper ‘pepper’, or of Dutch Pijper, an occupational name for a piper.
Altered form of North German   Pieper   1, a Low German form of   Pfeiffer  .

nameMeanings.name: VAN
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: LOON
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized form of German   Kuhn   or Dutch   Koen  .
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 管, 官 and 灌, see   Guan   2-4.

variants.name: Kuhn
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Hans, Wolfgang, Fritz, Gerhard, Manfred, Dieter, Erwin, Helmut, Arno, Gunther.

German: from the personal name Kuno, a short form of Kunrat (see Konrad ). The German word kühn, meaning ‘bold’, may have influenced the popularity of this short form, but is not necessarily the immediate source of it.
German (Kühn): see Kuehn .

nameMeanings.name: ou
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

variants.name: Ow
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
Chinese:
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou Yang
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ming, Wei, Hui, Jian, Hong, Chieh, Chu, Xin, Zhuo, Baoping, Biao, Bin, Hai, Pu, Quan, Tuan.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐陽, from Ou-Yu (歐餘), the name of a hill in Wucheng (in present-day Zhejiang province), which was granted to Ti, the second son of king Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ) in the state of Yue. Most of his descendants lived to the south of the hill called Ou-Yu (歐餘). The southern side of a hill was called Yang (陽) in ancient Chinese; the surname Ou-Yang (歐陽), literally means ‘the south of the Ou-Yu (歐餘) hill’. See also   Ou   1.

nameMeanings.name: Constance
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval form of the Late Latin name Constantia, which is either a feminine derivative of Constans ( see Constant ) or an abstract noun meaning ‘constancy’. This was a popular name among the Normans, and was borne by, amongst others, the formidable Constance of Sicily ( 1158–98 ), wife of the Emperor Henry VI .

en.lifeSummary: Constance VAN IEPER VAN LOON ou d'Ypres was born about 1026, in Ieper, West Flanders, Belgium. She had at least 4 sons and 2 daughters with Wauthier De Bailleul. She died in 1094, in Bailleul, Somme, Hauts-de-France, France, at the age of 69.

fr.lifeSummary: Constance VAN IEPER VAN LOON ou d'Ypres est née environ 1026, à Ieper, Flandre occidentale, Belgique. Elle a eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles avec Wauthier De Bailleul. Elle est décédée en 1094, à Bailleul, Somme, Hauts-de-France, France, à l’âge de 69 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Constance VAN IEPER VAN LOON ou d'Ypres wurde um 1026 in Ieper, West-Flandern, Belgien geboren. Sie hatte mit Wauthier De Bailleul mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1094 im Alter von 69 Jahren in Bailleul, Somme, Hauts-de-France, Frankreich.

it.lifeSummary: Constance VAN IEPER VAN LOON ou d'Ypres è nata il circa 1026 nel Ieper, Fiandra Occidentale, Belgio. Lei avuto almeno 4 figli e 2 figlie con Wauthier De Bailleul. Morì nel 1094, nel Bailleul, Somme, Hauts-de-France, Francia, all'età di 69 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Constance VAN IEPER VAN LOON ou d'Ypresは約1026年にベルギーウエスト-vラアンデレンイエペルで生まれた。彼女はWauthier De Bailleulとの間に少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1094年にフランスハウトス-デ-フランスソンメベイレウルで69歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Constance VAN IEPER VAN LOON ou d'Ypres(은)는 대략 1026년 벨기에 웨스트-블라아더 이퍼에서 태어났다. 그녀는 Wauthier De Bailleul(와)과 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1094년 프랑스 하츠-데-프랜스 솜 베이레우에서 69세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Constance VAN IEPER VAN LOON ou d'Ypres nasceu aproximadamente 1026, em Ieper, Flandres Ocidental, Bélgica. Ela teve pelo menos 4 filhos e 2 filhas com Wauthier De Bailleul. Ela faleceu em 1094, em Bailleul, Somme, Hauts-de-France, França, com 69 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Constance VAN IEPER VAN LOON ou d'Ypres родилась около 1026 г. в местности иепир, вест-влаандирен, Бельгия. У неё было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери от Wauthier De Bailleul. Она умерла в 1094 г. в местности беиллеул, сомм, хауц-де-фрэнс, Франция в возрасте 69 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Constance VAN IEPER VAN LOON ou d'Ypres nació aproximadamente 1026, en Ieper, Flandes Occidental, Bélgica. Tuvo por lo menos 4 hijos y 2 hijas con Wauthier De Bailleul. Murió en 1094, en Bailleul, Somme, Hauts-de-France, Francia, a la edad de 69 años.
